runtime_safety.mechanism: Tool.define() calls wrap() which overrides execute with Effect.gen(...).pipe(Effect.orDie, Effect.withSpan(...)). This runs at runtime regardless of the tool file's own content.

files_with_orDie_in_own_file.verdict: Double-wrapped — their own .pipe(Effect.orDie) is redundant but convention-consistent

files_missing_orDie_in_own_file.verdict: Safe — these use Effect.sync or Effect.succeed with no yield* inside, so no errors can escape. tool.ts wrap still applies orDie at runtime.
files_missing_orDie_in_own_file.detail: These tools are synchronous and cannot produce Effect errors. Effect.orDie on Effect.sync is a no-op.

cluster_a_tool_define_inference_failure.description: Effect<{...execute: (...) => Effect<..., never, Service>}, never, Service> is not assignable to Effect<Init<Parameters, Metadata>, never, Service>
cluster_a_tool_define_inference_failure.root_cause: Def.execute is typed as Effect.Effect<ExecuteResult<M>> (unconstrained, unknown/unknown). Tool files return Effect<ExecuteResult<M>, never, AppFileSystem.Service | InstanceState.Service>. In Effect v4, Effect type parameters may be invariant, so Effect<A, never, R> is not assignable to Effect<A, unknown, unknown>. This causes Init → DefWithoutID type inference to fail.

affected_files.note: Error: Metadata shape mismatch, not service/error channel

cluster_a_tool_define_inference_failure.fix_pattern: Cast init to Effect<Init<...>, never, R> or wrap the execute function's return type to narrow to never, never before tool.ts wrap.

cluster_b_app_runtime_layer_remainder.message: Layer<..., never, Service | Service> is not assignable to Layer<..., never, never>
cluster_b_app_runtime_layer_remainder.root_cause: One of the layers in AppLayer (Layer.mergeAll) has a remaining requirement of Service | Service instead of never. Likely InstanceLayer.layer, Observability.layer, or DatabaseConfig.defaultLayer.
cluster_b_app_runtime_layer_remainder.fix_pattern: Add .pipe(Layer.provideMerge(...)) for the missing service, or cast with as never

cluster_c_test_file_layer_remainder.message: Layer<..., never, Service | Service> is not assignable to Layer<..., never, never>
cluster_c_test_file_layer_remainder.root_cause: Test files provide layers with remaining service requirements to ManagedRuntime.make or layer piping that expects never requirements.
cluster_c_test_file_layer_remainder.fix_pattern: Add Layer.provideMerge for the missing services, or cast Layer with as never

cluster_d_read_lib_multi_error.root_cause: Effect.fnUntraced generator returns Generator<Effect<void, never, never>, void, any> not assignable to Effect<void, never, never>. Plus fs.readDirectory and fs.stat don't exist on AppFileSystem.Service type.
cluster_d_read_lib_multi_error.fix_pattern: Fix Effect.fnUntraced return type annotation. Use correct AppFileSystem API methods.

cluster_e_read_source_unknown_assignment.root_cause: Values read from JSON or Record<string, unknown> are 'unknown' type and being assigned to 'string' typed fields without narrowing.
cluster_e_read_source_unknown_assignment.fix_pattern: Add type assertions or runtime narrowing checks

cluster_f_schema_literal_argument_count.root_cause: Schema.Literal(...) with multiple arguments expects array in Effect v4 beta: Schema.Literals([...]) instead of Schema.Literal(a, b, c)
cluster_f_schema_literal_argument_count.fix_pattern: Replace Schema.Literal(a, b, c) with Schema.Literals([a, b, c])

files_with_unstaged_orDie_fixes.detail: These files already have the .pipe(Effect.orDie) fix in unstaged changes waiting to be committed

smoking_guns.gun_1: Def.execute return type is Effect.Effect<ExecuteResult<M>> (unconstrained), but tools return Effect<..., never, Service>. Effect v4 appears invariant in type params, causing Init inference to fail at ~14 Tool.define callsites.
smoking_guns.gun_2: coordination.ts yields ensureCoordinationTables() which returns Effect<void, DatabaseError, Adapter.Service> in the outer Effect.gen (not inside execute). This poisons the error channel for Tool.define's init parameter.
smoking_guns.gun_3: app-runtime.ts Layer.mergeAll accumulates Service | Service requirements instead of never. The layer with non-never remainder needs to be provided or merged away.
smoking_guns.gun_4: read-lib.ts uses fs.readDirectory and fs.stat which don't exist on AppFileSystem.Service. The walkDirectory Effect.fnUntraced generator also has an incompatible return type.

recommended_fix_pattern.for_ts2345_tool_define: At the Tool.define callsites, the outer Effect.gen must resolve to never in the error channel. Fix: if the outer gen yields anything that can fail (like ensureCoordinationTables()), add .pipe(Effect.orDie). For the Init type inference issue on the returned object, the fix is in tool.ts — either widen Def.execute's return to covariant Effect params or add an explicit type cast in the wrap function.
recommended_fix_pattern.for_sync_tools_missing_orDie: Adding .pipe(Effect.orDie) is unnecessary for Effect.sync/Effect.succeed (no error possible), but can be added for consistency if desired.
recommended_fix_pattern.for_app_runtime_layer: Identify which merged layer has the Service | Service remainder and provide it with Layer.provideMerge(...) or cast with Layer as never.
recommended_fix_pattern.for_schema_literal: Replace Schema.Literal(a, b, c, d) with Schema.Literals([a, b, c, d]) in all affected files.
recommended_fix_pattern.for_test_layers: Add Layer.provideMerge for remaining services in test layer construction, or cast Layer with as unknown as Layer<..., never, never>.

pre_existing_findings_relevant.summary: [typescript] Schema.String.annotations is not a function — tool-failure.ts crashes during import
[typescript] 10 tool files still use Schema.String.annotations({...}) which is a runtime error
